query.text: What is the current classification of Pluto in the solar system?

document.content: Pluto is the ninth planet in our solar system, discovered by Clyde Tombaugh in 1930. It orbits the Sun beyond Neptune at an average distance of 5.9 billion kilometers.
In August 2006, the International Astronomical Union voted to reclassify Pluto. It no longer meets the three criteria for full planetary status and is now called a dwarf planet.

Pluto is classified as a dwarf planet and the largest known trans-Neptunian object in the Kuiper Belt. NASA's New Horizons mission completed its flyby in July 2015, revealing mountains and nitrogen ice plains.

query.text: What is the recommended daily vitamin D intake for healthy adults?

document.content: The recommended daily intake of vitamin D is 200 IU for adults up to age 50, based on the 1997 Dietary Reference Intakes established by the National Academy of Sciences.
The 2010 Institute of Medicine report revised vitamin D recommendations upward to 600 IU daily for adults aged 1 to 70 and 800 IU for those over 70 years of age.
Current Endocrine Society guidelines recommend 1500 to 2000 IU of vitamin D daily for adults at risk of deficiency, with a tolerable upper intake level of 4000 IU per day.

query.text: What is the estimated total human population on Earth?

document.content: The world population reached 6 billion in October 1999, marking a milestone in human population growth that had doubled in just 40 years since 1960.
Global population surpassed 7 billion people in October 2011, with the United Nations projecting continued growth driven primarily by increases in sub-Saharan Africa.
The world population reached 8 billion in November 2022. UN projections estimate approximately 9.7 billion by 2050, with population growth slowing as fertility rates decline globally.
